issues.objection: A cynical writer's mocking gloss of 7.22 would target either (a) 'just commit to your stack, bro' or (b) 'work harder, but spiritual.' The engineering layer forecloses both: the falsifiability field explicitly rejects the soft reading; the counter_example carves out where the verse does not apply. The quotable_line ("The framework is the proximate cause; the customer-need-and-substrate is the actual granter — the labhate is real, the a…") is concrete enough to resist parody. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims Krishna is the actual granter (mayaiva vihitān); engineering analog operates at proximate-vs-upstream attribution scope (the framework is the vehicle; the customer-need-and-substrate is what actually grants the result). Counter-example carves out accidental success without disciplined practice.

notes: Cynical-writer test for 7.22: identify the parody-handle. The verse does not give one — the engineering layer is anchored to specific tooling and configurations; the quotable line is testable on the artifact, not on affect. PASS.
